QF.id: VIII Introduction to Clearing and Settlement System_73

question.text: The net worth requirement for a clearing member who clears and settles trades for other trading members in the derivatives segment is:

answer.text: At least Rs. 300 lakhs (Rs. 3 crore), with an additional deposit of Rs. 50 lakhs and Rs. 10 lakhs per additional TM
At least Rs. 100 lakhs (Rs. 1 crore) with no additional deposits
At least Rs. 500 lakhs (Rs. 5 crore) with deposits based on volume
No specific net worth requirement; only margins are required

explanation.text: **The Logic:** CMs who clear for other TMs need net worth ≥ Rs. 300 lakhs (Rs. 3 Cr), deposit of Rs. 50 lakhs to CC (part of security deposit), and additional Rs. 10 lakhs per additional TM. SCMs (clearing only own trades) need Rs. 100 lakhs. **The Trap:** Rs. 300 lakhs is for CMs clearing for others. SCMs need only Rs. 100 lakhs. Rs. 50 lakhs is a deposit, not net worth. Don't confuse the two. **Cross-Exam Context:** CM eligibility norms are regulatory facts tested in Series VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** CM = Rs. 3 Cr net worth + Rs. 50L deposit + Rs. 10L per TM. SCM = Rs. 1 Cr net worth.
